description:
## Problem

DSPy prompt rendering is currently hardcoded in `ChatAdapter`. Users can't customize how prompts are built beyond setting the instruction string. BAML gives users full Jinja control over their prompts - we should too.

## What Users Should Be Able To Customize

### 1. Instruction with Access to Inputs
The instruction should be a Jinja template, not just a static string. Users may want to:
- Reference input field values directly in the instruction
- Use conditionals based on input values
- Apply filters/formatting to values

### 2. How Input Values Are Formatted
When an input field value is inserted into the prompt, users may want to transform it:
- Truncate long strings
- Format dates in specific ways
- Serialize objects as JSON/YAML/custom format

### 3. How Demos Are Rendered
Few-shot examples are currently formatted with hardcoded structure. Users may want:
- Different formatting per use case
- Custom separators or labels
- Conditional inclusion of fields

### 4. How Output Schema Is Presented
BAML has `ctx.output_format` which auto-renders the expected output structure. Users should be able to:
- Control where/how this appears in the prompt
- Customize the format (JSON schema vs type hints vs prose)

## Constraints

- **Compile-time validation**: Invalid Jinja should fail at compile time, not runtime
- **Output fields don't need format adapters**: Parsing is already handled by jsonish coercer; output fields use alias/description for schema, check/assert for validation
- **Leverage existing BAML infra**: We have `internal-baml-jinja` with minijinja already

## What BAML Provides (for reference)

- `ctx.output_format` - renders output schema into prompt
- `template_string` - reusable template blocks
- Jinja filters: `format(type="json"|"yaml"|"toon")`, `regex_match`, `truncate`, etc.
- Full Jinja: conditionals, loops, variable access

## Current State

- `ChatAdapter::format_system_message()` - hardcoded prompt structure
- `ChatAdapter::format_demo_typed()` - hardcoded demo format
- Instruction is just a string, no templating
- No way to customize field value formatting

## References
- BAML ctx.output_format: https://docs.boundaryml.com/ref/prompt-syntax/ctx-output-format
- BAML Jinja filters: https://docs.boundaryml.com/ref/prompt-syntax/jinja-filters
- BAML template_string: https://docs.boundaryml.com/ref/baml/template-string
- Current adapter: `crates/dspy-rs/src/adapter/chat.rs`

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
Code pointers (as of 2026-01-27):
- Template entry points: `crates/dspy-rs/src/adapter/chat.rs` `format_system_message_typed_with_instruction`, `format_user_message_typed`, `format_demo_typed`.
- Jinja env: `crate::baml_bridge::jsonish::jinja_helpers::get_env()` used in `render_template`/`render_template_with_ws`.
- Context building: `build_template_context_base`, `add_template_inputs`, `add_template_outputs` in `adapter/chat.rs`.
- Default templates: `crates/dspy-rs/src/adapter/templates.rs` (system/user/assistant).
- Field helpers: `crates/dspy-rs/src/adapter/jinja_types.rs` exposes `JinjaField` + `JinjaBamlValue` with `.render()`.
- Signature hooks: `core/signature.rs` already exposes `system_template` and `user_template` for per-signature overrides.
Likely missing for "full" templating: allow instruction text to be a Jinja template rendered with the input ctx, plus demo/assistant template override hooks (mirroring system/user hooks).

title: Full Jinja templating for DSPy prompts (instruction, demos, fields)
